papers.title: OMEGA: Optimizing Machine Learning by Evaluating Generated Algorithms
papers.innovation: A full, end-to-end framework for automating AI research and ML development, from idea generation to executable code. It leverages structured meta-prompt engineering and executable code generation to build AI that builds AI.
papers.benchmarks: Implied evaluation of generated algorithms, focusing on the optimization and performance of ML models developed through the system.
papers.use_case: Automating ML model development, accelerating AI research, MLOps pipeline automation, and generating executable code for various machine learning tasks, significantly boosting productivity for AI engineers.

papers.title: Operating-Layer Controls for Onchain Language-Model Agents Under Real Capital
papers.innovation: Demonstrates and studies the reliability of autonomous language-model agents in a real-world, high-stakes financial environment (trading real ETH). Focuses on operating-layer controls for validated tool actions and user mandate translation under actual capital.
papers.benchmarks: Evaluated through a 21-day deployment (DX Terminal Pro) involving 3,505 user-funded agents trading real ETH in a bounded onchain market.
papers.use_case: Building and deploying robust, reliable, and auditable autonomous agents for critical applications such as decentralized finance, automated trading, supply chain management, and other high-stakes domains where agents interact with real capital or critical systems.

papers.title: Evaluating Strategic Reasoning in Forecasting Agents
papers.innovation: Introduces Bench to the Future 2 (BTF-2), a novel benchmark designed to evaluate not just the accuracy but also the strategic reasoning capabilities of forecasting agents. It provides a reproducible environment with a frozen research corpus to understand 'why' some forecasters are more accurate.
papers.benchmarks: BTF-2, consisting of 1,417 'pastcasting' questions with a frozen 15M-document research corpus, enabling reproducible research and offline forecasting.
papers.use_case: Developing and assessing advanced forecasting agents, improving the transparency and interpretability of predictive AI systems, and designing more insightful evaluation methodologies for complex AI behaviors beyond simple accuracy metrics.
